notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: OSubscription exposes ListSubscriptions only for Subscription in v65.110.0. The request requires compartmentId plus exactly one of planNumber, subscriptionId, or buyerEmail, and the response body is []SubscriptionSummary.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a matching OSubscription Subscription resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime is narrower than the visible SDK surface: the published kind is query-backed and observe-only, keeps create/update/delete/get out of scope, and projects a unique list match only when the selector query returns exactly one SubscriptionSummary.

SubscriptionSummary does not expose a clean top-level OCID or the request filter fields. The current rollout therefore leaves status.status.ocid empty, mirrors the raw sdkStatus string, and documents the identity and lifecycle taxonomy gaps explicitly in repo-authored formal metadata.

The list request also exposes x-one-gateway-subscription-id and x-one-origin-region headers. The published runtime surfaces those request knobs explicitly in spec so callers can satisfy access requirements without silently dropping the headers.
The osubsubscription/Subscription rollout must remain explicitly group-qualified and distinct from self/Subscription and ons/Subscription across formal, docs, package, and catalog surfaces.
